segments.paradiso-29-g27: A simple nominative noun phrase listing two metaphysical principles. 'Forma' is used in the abstract/essential sense (the Form, not mere shape); 'e' coordinates the two nouns; 'materia' names the material principle, matching 'forma' as a paired subject.
segments.paradiso-29-g28: 'Congiunte' and 'purette' are adjectival/participial modifiers agreeing with the feminine nouns 'Forma e materia' (hence plural/feminine). 'Congiunte' = 'joined/combined'; 'purette' is a poetic/archaic adjective (diminutive/intoned form) meaning 'purified' or 'pure'.
segments.paradiso-29-g29: 'Usciro' is an archaic/formal past tense (passato remoto) meaning 'they issued forth' (modern Italian: uscirono). The construction 'usciro ad esser' uses the preposition 'ad' + infinitive (a poetic infinitive-circumstance) meaning 'came forth to be'. 'Che' introduces a relative/explanatory clause; 'avia' is an archaic imperfect of 'avere' meaning 'had' (here 'had no flaw'), so 'non avia fallo' = 'had no fault'.
segments.paradiso-29-g30: A simile introduced by 'come' ('like/as'). 'd’ arco tricordo' = 'from/of a three‑stringed bow' with 'tricordo' qualifying 'arco'; 'tre saette' is the image's object ('three arrows'). Word order is the normal poetic order for a simile.
segments.paradiso-29-g31: 'E come in vetro,' begins a comparative simile ('And as in glass'), where 'come' introduces the likeness and 'in vetro' names the medium. Word order places the medium before the rest of the simile for poetic emphasis.
segments.paradiso-29-g32: Continues the simile listing alternative media: 'in ambra o in cristallo'. 'Raggio resplende' is present indicative (a vivid, timeless present) meaning 'a ray shines'. 'Sì' is an intensifier ('so/so much') linking to the result clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-29-g33: 'Che' introduces the result clause ('so... that'). 'Dal venire a l'esser' is a poetic expression using infinitives as nouns meaning 'from coming to being'; 'dal' = 'from the'. 'Tutto non è intervallo' uses 'tutto' as 'entirely/completely' and the negative construction to say 'there is no interval at all.'
segments.paradiso-29-g34: 'Così' resumes the comparison ('thus/so'), and the subject '’l triforme effetto del suo sire' = 'the three‑fold effect of its Lord' (Sire = God). 'Ne l'esser suo' = 'in its being' ('ne' = 'in'). 'Raggiò' is a poetic passato remoto meaning 'shone/radiated'. Adverbials 'insieme tutto' = 'all at once/completely' and 'sanza distinzïone' = 'without distinction' modify the verb; 'in essordire' = 'in beginning to appear/come into being' (infinitive used as a substantive adverbial).
segments.paradiso-29-g35: Concreato is a past participle placed before the finite verb (poetic inversion) and here means “made concrete/real.” Fu is the perfect/preterite of essere used with the participle to form a passive-like statement (“was made”); ordine e costrutto are the nominative subjects. A le sustanze uses the preposition a + the article (le) to mark the indirect/beneficiary phrase “for/to the substances.”
segments.paradiso-29-g36: E is the simple conjunction “and.” Quelle (“those”) refers back to the substances; furon is the archaic poetic form of furono (preterite of essere) meaning “they were.” Cima is a predicative noun (“the summit/top”). The relative clause nel mondo in che ... modifies cima: nel mondo = “in the world,” in che = “in which,” puro atto = the philosophical phrase “pure act,” and fu produtto is another past passive construction meaning “was produced,” with produtto an older spelling of prodotto.
segments.paradiso-29-g37: This clause is straightforward S‑V‑O: pura potenza (“pure potency” or potentiality) is the subject; tenne is the preterite of tenere meaning “held/occupied”; la parte ima is the direct object/predicate meaning “the lowest part,” with ima an archaic adjective meaning “lowest.” The order emphasizes the contrast between ‘pure potency’ and the ‘pure act’ mentioned above.
segments.paradiso-29-g38: Nel mezzo is an adverbial phrase “in the middle.” Strinse is the preterite of stringere used metaphorically (“bound, clasped, tightened”). The phrase potenza con atto names the two things being joined (power together with act); con links them (“with”). Tal vime is the instrument/means: tal = “such,” vime (plural of vima) literally “osiers/wicker” used metaphorically as “ties” or “bands.” Word order places the binding action centrally and images the union of power and act.
segments.paradiso-29-g39: Che introduces a result or relative clause (“such that/so that”). Già mai non is a negative emphatic sequence (già + mai + non) meaning effectively “never” or “never at all.” Si is the reflexive/passive clitic used to make the verb impersonal/passive; divima is an archaic/poetic form meaning “is divided” (here a subjunctive/passive sense). The whole clause says these bonds are never split/separated.
segments.paradiso-29-g40: Subject + locative + verb: Ieronimo is the subject, vi is a locative adverb meaning 'there', and scrisse is passato remoto (simple past) 'wrote'. Lungò tratto ('lungo tratto') is an adverbial phrase 'at length/for a long stretch' modifying scrisse. Di secoli de li angeli creati is a genitive/partitive phrase 'about the ages/centuries of the angels created' (de is an archaic form of di; creati is a post‑nominal past participle). Anzi che introduces a temporal subordinate 'before', and fosse fatto is a subjunctive passive ('fosse' = imperfect subjunctive of essere + fatto = past participle of fare) meaning 'were made' or 'had been made'.
segments.paradiso-29-g41: Contrast clause: ma 'but' introduces the contrast. Questo vero is a demonstrative noun phrase 'this truth'. È scritto is a present passive ('is written'); in molti lati means 'in many places' (lati used idiomatically as 'places' or 'directions'). Da li scrittor de lo Spirito Santo is an agent phrase with da = 'by' and de lo = archaic 'of the' — 'by the writers of the Holy Spirit'.
segments.paradiso-29-g42: Coordinating clause with an emphatic subject: e 'and' + tu (subject) + te (emphatic/reflexive pronoun) precedes the clitic ne (n') meaning 'of it' or 'about it' attached to avvedrai, future indicative 'you will perceive/notice'. Se is the conditional 'if' here (archaic/standard conjunction), bene 'well' modifies agguati; agguati (literally 'ambush'/'lying in wait') is used idiomatically: 'se bene agguati' = 'if you have well scrutinized / if you look closely'.
segments.paradiso-29-g43: And clause: e anche 'and also' links the thought. La ragione is the grammatical subject 'reason'. Il here is a neutral/pronominal il referring back to the previously stated point (literally 'it' or 'that'), followed by vede, present indicative 'sees' but best rendered 'understands/perceives'. Alquanto is an adverb meaning 'to some extent' or 'somewhat'.
segments.paradiso-29-g44: Content clause introduced by che 'that': non concederebbe is a conditional ('would not concede/allow'). The following che introduces a subjunctive clause (typical after verbs of doubting/denial): '’ motori' shows elision of the initial vowel/article (i motori), 'motori' means the 'movers' or driving forces (the philosophical 'prime movers'). Sanza is the archaic form of senza 'without'; sua perfezion ('their own perfection') uses the possessive sua in a distributive/possessive sense. Fosser is the archaic imperfect subjunctive plural (modern fossero) of essere, required after concederebbe che; cotanto means 'to such an extent' or 'so much'.
segments.paradiso-29-g81: Voi (2nd-person plural pronoun) + non + andate (present indicative, 2nd pl. of andare) forms the simple negative 'you do not go'. Giù is an adverb 'down' placed after the verb, and per + un sentiero is a prepositional phrase 'by/along a path' (per here expresses means). Filosofando is a gerund (present participle used adverbially) meaning 'by philosophizing' and specifies the manner/means that you do not use.
segments.paradiso-29-g82: Tanto is an adverb 'so much/so greatly' modifying trasporta; vi is the clitic indirect/object pronoun 'you' (or 'to you') that combines with trasporta (3rd sing. present of trasportare). The article l’ elides before amor; 'amor de l’apparenza' = 'the love of appearance' (de = of). E links the second coordinated noun phrase 'l suo pensiero' ('its/that one's thought'), where l' is the elided article and suo is the possessive modifying pensiero.
segments.paradiso-29-g83: E ancor = 'and yet/still' (ancor = ancora). Questo qua sù is a demonstrative phrase 'this here above' (qua sù = on this side / up here). Si comporta is a reflexive verb in the present indicative 'behaves itself' or 'conducts itself'; con men disdegno = 'with less disdain' (men = meno, comparative). Che functions as a comparative 'than' linking the clause che quando è posposta la divina Scrittura — quando è posposta is present passive (è + past participle posposta) meaning 'is set aside/postponed' — and the final o quando è torta gives an alternative 'or when it is twisted/perverted' (torta = predicative adjective).
segments.paradiso-29-g84: Non vi si pensa is an impersonal construction with si ('one/people do not think there'); vi is locative 'there' or 'in that matter'. Quanto introduces a clause of extent 'how much' modifying sangue costa ('how much blood it costs'), where costa is present indicative 3rd sing. of costare. Seminarla is the infinitive 'to sow' with the clitic la attached ('to sow it'); nel mondo is the locative 'in the world'. The coordination e quanto piace introduces another 'how much' clause: piace is 3rd-singular present 'it pleases' (here 'it pleases whoever...'), chi is 'who'/'he who', umilmente an adverb 'humbly', con essa 'with it', and s'accosta is reflexive present indicative 3rd sing. 'approaches himself/approaches (himself) to it'.
segments.paradiso-29-g85: Per + infinitive apparer (archaic infinitive of apparire) means 'in order to appear' or 'to seem'. Ciascun 'each one' is subject; s'ingegna is a reflexive present 'applies himself / contrives'. E face is an archaic form of fa (3rd sing. present of fare) meaning 'and makes'; sue invenzioni = 'his (own) inventions' with sue as the feminine plural possessive modifying invenzioni.
segments.paradiso-29-g86: E quelle 'and those' (feminine plural, referring back to 'sue invenzioni') son (sono) = 'are' and trascorse is a past participle used predicatively; da' predicanti uses da (here 'by') to mark the agent in a passive-like construction 'by the preachers'. The second coordinate clause 'e 'l Vangelio si tace' has the definite article l' + Vangelio 'the Gospel' as subject and si tace is a reflexive/passive-impersonal construction 'is silent' or 'keeps silent', i.e. the Gospel is neglected or made silent.
segments.paradiso-29-g45: Or sai tu places the verb before the subject for emphasis: 'Or' = 'now'; 'sai' is 2nd-person singular present indicative of sapere ('you know'). The clause 'dove e quando questi amori furon creati e come' is a substantive interrogative/relative content clause (where and when ... and how) whose verb 'furon creati' is passato remoto (3rd plural) agreeing with the plural subject 'questi amori' and meaning 'were created'.
segments.paradiso-29-g46: 'sì che' introduces a result clause ('so that'); 'spenti' is a past participle used predicatively and linked to the finite verb 'son' (present plural of essere) to form 'are extinguished'. 'Nel tuo disïo' is a prepositional phrase 'in your desire' (note the diaeresis in disïo indicating vowel separation). Word order 'già son' is normal poetic inversion: 'they are already'.
segments.paradiso-29-g47: Né is a simple negative coordinating conjunction 'nor'. Giugneriesi is an archaic/poetic conditional form meaning 'you would arrive/reach' (second person), so the phrase reads 'nor would you reach...'. Because the provided sense list for 'giugneriesi' is not applicable, its sense index is given as -1.
segments.paradiso-29-g48: Numerando is a gerund from numerare meaning 'by counting' or 'while counting' and functions adverbially to show the manner of the action expressed by the (elliptical) main verb.
segments.paradiso-29-g49: Al venti here means 'to/at twenty' (i.e. 'reach twenty') with the contracted preposition+article 'al'. 'Sì tosto' is an adverbial intensifier meaning 'so soon/so quickly' modifying the clause.
segments.paradiso-29-g50: Come introduces a comparison 'as' followed by the clause 'de li angeli parte turbò il suggetto d’i vostri alimenti.' 'De li angeli parte' means 'a part of the angels' (partitive subject). 'Turbò' is passato remoto (3rd sing.) of turbare 'disturbed'. 'Il suggetto d’i vostri alimenti' is the direct object: 'the subject/source of your aliment(s)/sustenance.' Note that the provided senses for 'angeli' do not match the noun here, so its sense index is -1.
segments.paradiso-29-g51: L’altra ('the other' feminine) is the subject and 'rimase' is passato remoto (3rd sing.) of rimanere, meaning 'remained' or 'stayed behind' — a simple past action describing what the other did.
segments.paradiso-29-g52: E cominciò: 'and began' (cominciò is passato remoto 3rd sing.). 'Quest’ arte' is the direct object ('this art'), and the relative clause 'che tu discerni' uses the present indicative ('you discern') to indicate that the art is still recognizable to the addressee.
segments.paradiso-29-g53: 'Con tanto diletto' is an adverbial phrase ('with so much delight'), where 'tanto' intensifies 'diletto' and modifies the manner in which the art was begun.
segments.paradiso-29-g54: 'Che' introduces the result clause 'so much ... that ...'. 'Mai' with the negative 'non' yields 'never' here. 'Da circüir' = 'from encircling' (circüir = to encircle; the diaeresis marks vowel separation). 'Si diparte' is reflexive present 'does not part/leave itself', so the whole = 'that it never departs from encircling.'
segments.paradiso-29-g55: Syntactically 'Principio del cader' ('the beginning of the fall') uses the infinitive 'cader' substantively. 'Fu' is passato remoto (3rd sg.) of essere 'was'. 'Il maladetto superbir' is the predicate nominative ('the accursed pride'); 'superbir' is an archaic form used substantively here (the provided sense list does not give the noun meaning, so its sense index is -1). The relative clause 'di colui che tu vedesti da tutti i pesi del mondo costretto' means 'of him whom you saw constrained by all the weights of the world' — 'vedesti' is past tense 'you saw' (the provided sense list is mismatched, so its index is -1) and 'costretto' is a past participle used as object complement ('seen constrained'). 'Da tutti i pesi del mondo' is an agent/causal phrase 'by/all the weights of the world.'
segments.paradiso-29-g1: Quando is a temporal conjunction 'when'. Ambedue is an emphatic adjective 'both' placed before the noun. Li figli is the masculine plural article + noun 'the sons/children'; di Latona is a simple genitive 'of Latona' (the mythological Leto) modifying 'figli'.
segments.paradiso-29-g2: Coperti is a past participle used adjectivally ('covered'); it agrees with 'figli' (understood masculine plural). Del is the contraction of di + il 'of the'. Montone and Libra are nouns naming constellations/signs; e = 'and'; the second preposition is the archaic form de = 'of'.
segments.paradiso-29-g3: Fanno is present indicative, 3rd person plural of fare 'make/do'. De l’orizzonte is a partitive/relational phrase 'of the horizon' (de + l’). Insieme 'together' modifies the verb; zona is a noun 'zone/band' that completes the object: 'they make a band of the horizon together.'
segments.paradiso-29-g4: This clause gives the extent: quant’ è = 'as much/as far as is' (quant’ = quant' abbreviated). Dal punto = 'from the point'; che introduces a relative clause 'that/where'; ’l cenìt is the article + 'zenith'; inlibra is a poetic/intransitive verb ('inlibrare') meaning 'to poise/put into balance' in the 3rd person singular present, so 'where the zenith poises itself.' The second half continues with infin che = 'until that' introducing the limit 'until one and the other from that girdle'.
segments.paradiso-29-g5: Cambiando is a present participle ('changing') that modifies the preceding clause and indicates the manner/circumstance: 'changing the hemisphere.' L’emisperio is the direct object 'the hemisphere' (here a celestial hemisphere).
segments.paradiso-29-g6: Si is the reflexive/clitic pronoun used with intransitive verbs; dilibra is 3rd person singular of the verb dilibrare ('to oscillate/veer'): together they mean 'is moved/oscillates' or 'is set apart' in this context. The short clause reports the effect produced by the preceding change.
segments.paradiso-29-g7: Tanto is an adverb here meaning 'so much' or 'to such a degree' and introduces the result clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-29-g8: Col is the contraction of con + il 'with the'; volto means 'face' (here Bëatrice's expression). Di riso is a genitive-like phrase 'of/with a smile' (riso = smile, not rice); dipinto is a past participle 'painted' used figuratively: 'with a face painted with a smile.'
segments.paradiso-29-g9: Si tacque is reflexive + passato remoto (tacque, 3rd sing.) of tacere 'to fall silent', so 'she fell silent.' Bëatrice is the subject (proper name) placed after the verb for poetic emphasis.
segments.paradiso-29-g10: Riguardando is a present participle of riguardare ('looking at/looking back at') and links to the main verb to show attendant action. Fiso (fixed) modifies 'riguardando' (i.e. 'looking fixedly'); nel punto 'at the point/spot'; che introduces a relative clause; m’ is the clitic for 'mi' (me) but the supplied sense list does not include the pronominal 'me' — here it functions as the object pronoun 'me'; avëa is the imperfect of avere 'had' and vinto is the past participle of vincere 'conquer/overcome', so 'the point that had overcome me.'
segments.paradiso-29-g11: Poi 'then' places the action in sequence. Cominciò is passato remoto, 3rd person singular of cominciare 'to begin', so 'she began.'
segments.paradiso-29-g12: Io dico is a simple present 'I say/I speak', with the subject pronoun Io given for emphasis (unusual in Italian unless emphatic or stylistic).
segments.paradiso-29-g13: E non dimando: e = 'and'; non = negation 'not'. Dimando here causes problems: the lemma/senses supplied (to ebb) do not match the sense in context; the phrase means 'I do not ask' (dimandare = to ask), and dimando is present form 'I ask' used negatively 'I do not ask.'
segments.paradiso-29-g14: Quel che = 'that which/what'; tu is the familiar 'you'; vuoli is an archaic/poetic form of volere 'you wish/want' (here 'you want'); udir is the infinitive 'to hear' (older form of udire). The supplied sense for vuoli (fly) is unrelated to this usage.
segments.paradiso-29-g15: Perch’ is the clitic/shortened perché meaning 'because' or 'for'; io is subject 'I'; l’ ho visto = 'I have seen it' (l' = direct object pronoun + ho 'I have', visto past participle). Là 'there' points to a place; ’ve is a reduced form of 'ove' (where) with clitic s' = si, and s’appunta here means 'is directed/comes together'—s' is reflexive clitic. Ogne ubi e ogne quando literally 'every where and every when'; ubi is an archaic word 'where', quando 'when'. The clause says 'I have seen it there where every place and every time converge.'
segments.paradiso-29-g16: Non per + infinitive expresses purpose negated: 'not for the purpose of.' Aver a sé 'to obtain for herself' (aver = infinitive 'to have/get', a sé = 'to herself'). Di bene acquisto is a noun phrase 'a gain/ acquisition of good' (acquisto = gain/profit here); the whole means 'not to acquire any good for herself.'
segments.paradiso-29-g17: Ch’ is a short form of che here meaning 'which/that' or 'for that' and introduces the explanatory clause. Esser non può = 'she cannot be' (esser = infinitive 'to be'; non = negation; può = 3rd person singular present of potere 'can/able to').
segments.paradiso-29-g18: Ma 'but'; perché here has a purposive sense 'so that' (not just 'because') and governs the subjunctive potesse. Suo splendore 'her/its splendour' is the subject of potesse (imperfect subjunctive of potere) meaning 'might be able.'
segments.paradiso-29-g19: Risplendendo is a present participle 'shining/ by shining' describing the manner in which the splendour acts; it links to the following infinitive dir.
segments.paradiso-29-g20: Dir is the infinitive 'to say'. Subsisto is quoted in Latin and functions as the content of what would be said: 'Subsisto' = 'I subsist/remain' (here a declaration of existence).
segments.paradiso-29-g21: In sua etternità di tempo fore: in + sua 'in its' etternità 'eternity'; di tempo 'of time' qualifies etternità; fore here is used poetically to mean 'outside' or 'beyond' (i.e. 'outside of time'). The whole phrase sets the ontological frame 'in its eternity, outside of time.'
segments.paradiso-29-g22: Fuor d’ogne altro comprender: fuor = 'outside' or 'beyond'; d’ = di 'of'; ogne altro = 'every other thing'; comprender is an infinitive meaning either 'to understand' or 'to include/encompass' — here it means 'to comprehend/bear within itself.'
segments.paradiso-29-g23: Come = 'as' or 'in the way that'; i is the archaic article clitic (here functioning like 'gli'/'a lui') and piacque is passato remoto of piacere 'to please'. The phrase 'come i piacque' = 'as it pleased Him' (i = to Him, indirect object).
segments.paradiso-29-g24: S’aperse is reflexive/passive-looking passato remoto of aprirsi 'to open oneself' (s’ = si); aperse = 'opened/began/revealed'. In nuovi amor 'into new loves' (in + plural noun); l’etterno amore = 'the eternal Love' (the subject); the sequence says that, as it pleased Him, the eternal Love opened itself into new loves.
segments.paradiso-29-g25: Né = 'nor/neither'; prima = 'before' or 'previously'; quasi = 'almost/as if'; torpente (torpente) is an adjective 'torpid/drowsy'; si + giacque: giacque is passato remoto (3rd sing.) of giacere 'to lie' and the clitic si gives a reflexive/impersonal flavour — 'she lay down / was laid' or 'she remained (as if) torpid'.
segments.paradiso-29-g26: Ché = 'for/because'; the coordinate negatives né prima né poscia mean 'neither before nor after/afterward'. Procedette is passato remoto of procedere 'proceeded' (3rd sing.). Lo discorrer 'the discourse/speech' (lo + infinitive used substantively); di Dio 'about/of God'; sovra quest’ acque = 'over/on these waters' (sovra = over/about; quest’ acque = these waters). The clause says the discourse of God did not proceed either before or after over these waters.
segments.paradiso-29-g123: Simple noun phrase: the definite article 'La' + adjectival modifier 'prima' before the noun 'luce'. Placing 'prima' before 'luce' gives a focal, almost titular emphasis — 'the first/primary light'.
segments.paradiso-29-g124: 'che' is the relative pronoun introducing the clause. 'tutta' (= wholly, throughout) modifies 'raia'; here 'raia' is a poetic/archaic word for a beam or radiance (Dante’s diction), so the phrase means that the light throws its radiance everywhere. Word order is compact and idiomatic to medieval Italian poetry.
segments.paradiso-29-g125: 'per tanti modi' = 'in so many ways' (per + plural noun = manner expression). 'in essa si recepe' is a passive/reflexive construction: 'si recepe' (archaic present of recepere/recepire) = 'is received' and 'essa' = 'in it' (a feminine pronoun referring back to some feminine entity); the clitic 'si' creates the impersonal/passive sense typical of Dante's syntax.
segments.paradiso-29-g126: 'quanti son li splendori' = 'as many as there are splendors' (quanti used in the sense 'as many as'). 'a chi s’appaia' = 'to whom it appears' with 'appaia' in the subjunctive (si + appareo/apparire): the subjunctive here expresses the general/indefinite 'to whoever it may appear'. The clitic 's’' is the reflexive/impersonal marker attached to the verb.
segments.paradiso-29-g127: 'Onde' is a discursive connective meaning 'hence' / 'therefore' introducing a consequence or explanation. It is an archaic marker frequently used by Dante to signal inference.
segments.paradiso-29-g128: 'però che' here functions as a causal conjunction ('because'/'since'). 'a l’atto che concepe' = 'at the act/moment that it conceives' (a + l’atto; 'concepe' = 3rd person present of concepire/conscepere in Dante’s orthography). 'segue l’affetto' = 'the affection follows' with 'segue' as simple present 'follows' and 'l’affetto' = 'the feeling/affection'. The whole clause describes a temporal/causal sequence.
segments.paradiso-29-g129: 'd’amar la dolcezza' = the noun phrase 'the sweetness of loving' (di + infinitive used as noun). 'diversamente' is an adverb modifying the verbs that follow. 'in essa ferve e tepe' uses two present verbs in parallel: 'ferve' (seethes/boils, figurative) and 'tepe' (is warm/warms), both 3rd-person singular and referring back to 'essa' (in it); the pairing gives a poetic sense of differing intensities of feeling inside.
segments.paradiso-29-g130: 'Vedi' is the second-person singular imperative ('see' / 'behold'). 'l’eccelso' is an abstract substantive formed from an adjective meaning 'the exalted/sublime (thing)'. 'omai' is an adverb meaning 'now' (Dante’s archaic usage), and the phrase continues with 'e la larghezza de l’etterno valor' = 'and the breadth of the eternal power/value' (de = old form of di). The sequence is an exhortation to observe the glory being described.
segments.paradiso-29-g131: 'poscia che' = 'since' / 'after' (introducing the explanatory clause). 'tanti speculi fatti s’ha' uses an impersonal/agentless construction: 'so many mirrors have been made' — 'speculi' = 'mirrors' (figurative), 'fatti' = past participle 'made', and 's’ha' (si + ha) is the medieval impersonal/passive auxiliary roughly 'there have been'. 'in che si spezza' = 'in which it is broken/shattered' with 'si spezza' as reflexive/passive ('is broken') referring back to the light/divine ray.
segments.paradiso-29-g132: 'uno' here means 'each one' or 'each' and is the subject. 'manendo' is the present participle/gerund 'remaining' (from manere), used here to mean 'remaining/staying'. 'in sé' = 'in itself' and 'come davanti' = 'as before' or 'as formerly'; the clause states that each mirror (or each resulting light) remains in itself just as it was previously.
segments.paradiso-29-g106: Ma = 'but'. tale modifies uccel and means 'such' (emphatic). 'nel' is the contraction of in + il = 'in the'. becchetto is a diminutive 'little beak/tip' (literal or figurative). s' is the reflexive/clitic si attached before the verb, and annida is 3rd-person singular present (s'annida) meaning 'nests/settles' (the reflexive verb form). Word order is straightforward: conjunction + adjective + noun + locative phrase + reflexive verb.
segments.paradiso-29-g107: che introduces the following clause ('that/which' or 'and that'). se is the conditional 'if' (here introducing an if-clause). '’l vulgo' = 'the common people' with the article elided; the second 'il' after vulgo is the object clitic (archaic spelling for lo: 'it/him') referring back to the thing seen. vedesse is the imperfect subjunctive of vedere ('should see/were to see'), used in this conditional clause.
segments.paradiso-29-g108: vederebbe is the conditional 'would see' (3rd sing.). la is the feminine article 'the' modifying perdonanza. perdonanza here means an indulgence/forgiveness granted (church sense). di ch'el si confida is a relative phrase 'of which he (or it) trusts itself/relies on' — ch' = che 'which/that', el = 'he/it' (referring back to the subject), si is the reflexive clitic, and confida (present indicative) means 'trusts/relies upon'.
segments.paradiso-29-g109: per cui = 'by/for which' or 'because of which' linking the previous clause to the result. tanta modifies stoltezza: 'such great foolishness'. in terra = 'on earth' / 'in the world'. crebbe is the past tense (passato remoto) 3rd person singular of crescere 'grew/arose'.
segments.paradiso-29-g110: che here is a simple connective 'that' introducing the continuation/explanation of how great the foolishness was; it links to the following clause.
segments.paradiso-29-g111: sanza is an archaic form of senza 'without'. prova here is a noun meaning 'proof/evidence' (not the verb). d' = di 'of'. alcun means 'any' (in negative contexts: 'any/any kind of'). testimonio means 'testimony/evidence'. Together: 'without any proof of testimony'.
segments.paradiso-29-g112: ad ogne = 'to every' (ad + each). promession is an archaic form meaning 'promise' or 'pledge'. si correrebbe is an impersonal/defective use: correrebbe is conditional 'would run' and si makes it impersonal ('people would rush'). Literally: 'they would run to every promise.'
segments.paradiso-29-g113: Di questo = 'from this' / 'because of this'. ingrassa is 3rd-person singular present of ingrassare 'fattens' (the pig gets fat). il porco Sant' Antonio = 'the pig of Saint Anthony' (il + porco; Sant' is apocopated sant' before a vowel). The construction states a cause: 'Because of this, Saint Anthony's pig fattens.'
segments.paradiso-29-g114: e altri assai = 'and many others' (assai = 'many / very'). che sono ancor più porci is a relative clause: 'who are even more piggish' — sono is 'are', ancor = 'still/even', più = 'more', porci here is pejorative 'pigs'/'filthy' used metaphorically.
segments.paradiso-29-g115: pagando is a gerund 'paying' that describes how they act. di moneta = 'with money/coin'. sanza = archaic 'without'. conio = 'coinage/minting' (the stamped impression on coins). The phrase criticizes paying with money that lacks proper coinage (i.e., base or unscrupulous money).
segments.paradiso-29-g116: Ma = 'but'. perché = 'because/since' introducing the reason for returning to the main line. siam is the archaic contraction of siamo (we are / auxiliary), here used with the past participle to form a perfect meaning 'we have'. digressi is the past participle 'digressed' (masculine plural), so siam digressi = 'we have digressed'. assai = 'very/much'.
segments.paradiso-29-g117: ritorci is an imperative/hortative form meaning 'let us turn back' or 'let us turn' (the original verb is reflexive: ritornare/ritorcersi-ish usage); li occhi = 'the eyes' (li is the masculine plural article clitic for occhi). oramai = 'now/at this point'. verso la dritta strada = 'toward the right/straight road' (dritta = straight/right, strada = road). The whole phrase is an exhortation to return the gaze to the correct path.
segments.paradiso-29-g118: sì che = 'so that' / 'with the result that'. la via = 'the way'. col is the contraction of con + il = 'with the'. tempo = 'time'. si raccorci is a reflexive/passive-like subjunctive or present-tense middle form meaning 'may be shortened' or 'is shortened' (raccorciarsi = 'to shorten'); overall: 'so that the way will be shortened with time.'
segments.paradiso-29-g119: Questa natura = 'this nature' (this order/structure). sì oltre = 'so far beyond' / 'to such an extent further'. s' is the reflexive clitic attached to ingrada; ingrada is an archaic/poetic verb form meaning 'enlarges/extends itself' or 'grows' (s'ingrada = 'extends itself'). in numero = 'into/with respect to number'.
segments.paradiso-29-g120: che introduces the result clause. mai non fu = 'never was' (mai = ever/never in negative context; fu = was, passato remoto). loquela = 'speech' or 'utterance'. né concetto mortal = 'nor mortal concept/thought' (concetto = idea/conception; mortal qualifies it as human/fallible). che tanto vada: che = 'that', tanto 'so far/so much', vada is subjunctive of andare ('go/proceed') meaning 'has ever gone so far'. Overall: 'that no mortal speech or thought has ever reached so far.'
segments.paradiso-29-g121: e se tu guardi = 'and if you (tu) look/consider'. quel che si revela = 'that which is revealed' (si revela = 'is revealed', a middle/passive construction). per Danïel = 'through/by Daniel' (i.e., in the prophecy of Daniel). The clause invites the reader to consult Daniel's revelation as confirmation.
segments.paradiso-29-g122: vedrai = future 2nd-person singular 'you will see'. che introduces the subordinate content. '’n' (here written n) is a contraction of in = 'in'. sue = 'its' (referring to Daniel's thousands). migliaia = 'thousands'. determinato numero = 'a determined/fixed number'. si cela is reflexive/middle 'hides itself / is hidden'. Literal sense: 'you will see that in his thousands a determinate number is concealed.'
segments.paradiso-29-g87: Un here is an indefinite pronoun meaning “someone” (not the numeral uno). Dice is present indicative “says”; che introduces the subordinate clause. La luna si ritorse uses the reflexive si with ritorse (passato remoto, 3rd sg), a poetic past meaning “turned away” or “recoiled”; ne la passion = poetic prepositional phrase meaning “in/during the Passion” (ne + definite article). E s’interpuose is another passato remoto, s’ + interpuose (s’interpose), so both verbs narrate a past action of the moon.
segments.paradiso-29-g88: Per che introduces the result/consequence (“so that/wherefore”). ’L lume del sol = “the light of the sun” (l = the, lume = light, del = of the). Giù non si porse is passato remoto porse (3rd sg) with reflexive si and negation non; giù “down” indicates direction — literally “did not present itself downward” = “did not reach down.”
segments.paradiso-29-g89: E mente is a short sentence: e = “and,” mente is a verb (from mentire) meaning “lies” (3rd sg present), not the noun mento “chin.” It introduces Dante’s correction/objection to the claim just reported.
segments.paradiso-29-g90: Ché = “because/for.” La luce si nascose uses si + nascose (passato remoto, 3rd sg) of nascóndere; here si nascose = “hid itself” (nascose as reflexive). Da sé = “from itself” (da = from, sé = itself) — a paradoxical poetic expression.
segments.paradiso-29-g91: Però = “therefore/so”; a li Spani e a l’Indi are indirect-object-like phrases meaning “to the Spaniards and to the Indians.” Come a’ Giudei introduces a comparison “as (it did) to the Jews.” Tale eclissi rispuose: rispuose is passato remoto 3rd sg “answered/responded/caused,” so the clause says it produced the same sort of eclipse for Spaniards and Indians as for the Jews.
segments.paradiso-29-g92: Non ha Fiorenza introduces a negative statement in present tense: “Florence does not have.” Tanti Lapi e Bindi quante … sets up a comparison (“as many Lapi and Bindi as …”). Sì fatte favole = “such made/fabricated tales” (sì = so/such, fatte = made). Per anno = “per year.” In pergamo si gridan quinci e quindi is an impersonal/passive se construction (si + 3pl gridan) meaning “are proclaimed/shouted on parchment here and there”; pergamo (parchment) is the medium.
segments.paradiso-29-g93: Sì che introduces the result clause (“so that”). Le pecorelle is a diminutive noun used figuratively: “the little sheep” (weak, gullible people).
segments.paradiso-29-g94: Che non sanno is a relative clause modifying le pecorelle: che = “who/that,” non sanno = present tense “do not know.”
segments.paradiso-29-g95: Tornan (present 3pl) del pasco = “return from the pasture.” Pasciute is a past participle used adjectivally “fed/satiated”; di vento is a figurative complement “on wind” meaning they are fed by empty air/vanity (i.e. fed on nothing).
segments.paradiso-29-g96: E non le scusa non veder lo danno is terse and idiomatic: non le scusa = “it does not excuse them” (scusa = present 3rd sg of scusare with clitic le = ‘them’); non veder lo danno = “not seeing the harm” (an infinitive clause describing the supposed excuse). Together: “and not seeing the harm does not excuse them.” Note the double non is emphatic in older Italian.
segments.paradiso-29-g97: Non disse is passato remoto (3rd sg) “did not say.” Cristo al suo primo convento = indirect object: “to Christ’s first assembly/convent.” The whole segment negates that Christ gave the instruction to that original group.
segments.paradiso-29-g98: ‘Andate’ is the 2nd person plural imperative (“Go!”) in direct speech — a command form. The quote begins with this imperative.
segments.paradiso-29-g99: E predicate is coordinate with Andate (another imperative, 2nd pl): “and preach.” Al mondo = “to the world.” Ciance is a plural noun meaning “idle talk/nonsense”; the quotation therefore says ‘preach nonsense to the world.’
segments.paradiso-29-g100: Ma = “but” introduces the contrast. Diede is passato remoto “gave” (3rd sg); lor = to them; verace fondamento = “true/genuine foundation” (adjective verace placed before noun for emphasis).
segments.paradiso-29-g101: E quel tanto sonò ne le sue guance = “and that resounded so much in his cheeks.” Sonò is passato remoto of sonare (“sounded/rang”); ne + le sue guance = poetic locative “in his cheeks” (ne = in). Quel tanto = “so much/that much.”
segments.paradiso-29-g102: Sì ch’ = “so that”; a pugnar = a + infinitive used to indicate purpose or action “to fight/engage in fighting” (pugnar infinitive). Per accender la fede de l’Evangelio is a purpose phrase “in order to kindle the faith of the Gospel.” Fero scudo e lance: fero (fierce/ferocious) qualifies scudo e lance (shield and lances) — a metaphor: they became weapons (shield and spear) for the faith. Note older forms/declensions and compressed word order typical of Dante.
segments.paradiso-29-g103: Ora si va is an impersonal present construction: “now one goes/people go.” Con motti e con iscede lists the instruments/manners: motti = witticisms/clever sayings; iscede (iscedere) means to withdraw or to make exits — here implying stagey departures or rhetorical moves. A predicare = purpose “to preach.”
segments.paradiso-29-g104: E pur che ben si rida is a concessive/conditional clause: pur che = “provided that/so long as”; ben = “well” (here ‘well’ in the sense of ‘to a good effect’); si rida is impersonal/subjunctive use of ridere (here Dante uses the subjunctive mood after pur che) meaning “one laughs/people laugh” — the sense: as long as they get a laugh, that suffices.
segments.paradiso-29-g105: Gonfia il cappuccio is present 3rd sg gonfia (inflate/puff out) with il cappuccio “the hood” (image of monastic pride); e più non si richiede = “and no more is requested/asked” (più non = no longer; si richiede is reflexive/passive sense “is asked/required”), i.e. once the hood is puffed up and a laugh is achieved they ask for nothing else.
segments.paradiso-29-g56: This whole clause states who the people are and why they were humble. 'Quelli che vedi qui' is a demonstrative + relative clause 'those whom you see here.' 'furon' is the archaic past-historical form (furono) meaning 'they were.' 'modesti a riconoscer sé' uses 'a' + infinitive to indicate disposition or purpose: 'modest in recognizing themselves / in acknowledging themselves.' 'da la bontate che li avea fatti a tanto intender presti' is a relative clause: 'from/by the goodness that had made them so quick to understand' — 'avea' is imperfect ('had'), 'fatti' is a past participle used with 'avea' ('had made'), and 'presti' is an adverb meaning 'quickly' or 'ready.'
segments.paradiso-29-g57: 'per che' introduces the consequence/reason: 'so/therefore.' 'le viste lor furo essaltate' is a passive construction in the past-historical (furo essaltate = were exalted), where 'le viste' means 'their visions/sights/insights.' 'con grazia illuminante e con lor merto' states the instruments/causes: 'by illuminating grace and by their merit.' Note the poetic order (object before auxiliary + participle) typical of Dante.
segments.paradiso-29-g58: 'si c’hanno' is an older clitic combination (si + ci/hanno) meaning they 'have in themselves' or 'possess' — here emphasizing possession within them. 'ferma e piena volontate' are two adjectives modifying 'volontate' (will/desire): 'a firm and full will.' The clause states a result of the exalted vision: they possess a firm, full will.
segments.paradiso-29-g59: This is a declarative by the poet-speaker: 'e non voglio che dubbi' — literally 'and I do not want that doubts [exist].' 'voglio' is present indicative 'I wish/ I do not want,' and 'che' introduces the subordinate idea (here elliptical: 'that there be doubts'). The clause is deliberately concise for the verse.
segments.paradiso-29-g60: 'ma sia certo' = 'but be certain' (or 'rather, let it be certain'). 'sia' is present subjunctive of 'essere' used as an exhortation or strong assertion ('let it be certain' / 'be assured'). 'ma' here introduces contrast/emphasis.
segments.paradiso-29-g61: This is the content of the certainty: 'che ricever la grazia è meritorio secondo che l’affetto l’è aperto.' 'ricever' is the infinitive (archaic truncation of 'ricevere') — 'to receive grace.' 'è meritorio' = 'is meritorious.' 'secondo che' means 'according as/depending on how,' and the final clause 'l’affetto l’è aperto' states the standard: 'the affection/ disposition is open' (note the duplicated clitic 'l’ ... l’è' used for prosody but meaning 'the affection is open').
segments.paradiso-29-g62: 'Omai' means 'by now' or 'at this point.' 'dintorno a questo consistorio' = 'around this consistory/council.' 'puoi contemplare assai' is straightforward present 'you can contemplate/see much' — 'assai' = 'much' or 'a great deal.'
segments.paradiso-29-g63: 'se le parole mie son ricolte' = 'if my words are gathered/collected.' 'se' = 'if,' 'le parole mie' = 'my words,' 'son' = poetic present 'are,' and 'ricolte' (past participle of 'ricolgere') implies 'well gathered, carefully chosen, or properly collected/arranged.'
segments.paradiso-29-g64: 'sanz’ altro aiutorio' is a contracted form of 'senza altro aiutorio': 'without any other aid/assistance.' 'sanz'' (senza) is a frequent poetic contraction; 'aiutorio' = 'help/aid.' The phrase qualifies the previous conditional: if my words are correct, you need no other help.
segments.paradiso-29-g65: 'Ma perché ’n terra per le vostre scole si legge' — 'but because on earth, in your schools, it is read/said/taught.' 'si legge' is impersonal/passive 'one reads/it is taught.' 'che l’angelica natura è tal' = 'that angelic nature is such.' The clause sets up a doctrinal point attributed to scholastic teaching.
segments.paradiso-29-g66: The relative clause 'che ’ntende e si ricorda e vole' lists three present-tense verbs describing angelic nature: 'it understands (intende), remembers itself (si ricorda), and wills (vole).' All verbs are present indicative and coordinated by 'e.'
segments.paradiso-29-g67: 'ancor dirò' = 'I will still say' or 'I will yet say.' 'ancor' = 'still/yet' and 'dirò' is future 'I will say'; Dante signals he will add a remark despite what the schools say.
segments.paradiso-29-g68: 'perché tu veggi pura la verità' — here 'perché' has the purposive sense 'so that,' and 'veggi' is the present subjunctive (optative/purposive) meaning 'you may see' or 'you see clearly.' 'pura la verità' = 'the truth clearly/purely.' The following relative 'che là giù si confonde' explains that truth is confused 'down there.'
segments.paradiso-29-g69: 'equivocando in sì fatta lettura' is a participial clause modifying 'si confonde' — 'equivocating/mistaking in such a mode of reading/interpretation.' 'equivocando' is a present participle/gerund indicating the manner of the confusion; 'sì fatta lettura' = 'such a reading/interpretation.'
segments.paradiso-29-g70: 'Queste sustanze' is a nominative subject: 'these substances/essences' (i.e., the souls/spirits Dante has been describing). The demonstrative plus noun sets the topic of the sentence.
segments.paradiso-29-g71: 'poi che fur gioconde de la faccia di Dio' = 'after/for since they were made joyful by the face of God.' 'poi che' = 'since/after,' 'fur' = past-historical 'were,' and 'gioconde' (pleasing/joyful) describes their condition produced 'de la faccia di Dio' (by God's presence/face).
segments.paradiso-29-g72: 'non volser viso da essa' is an older ordering meaning 'they did not turn their gaze away from it.' 'volser' is the archaic past form (volsero/voltare here 'turn'), 'viso' = gaze/face, 'da essa' = 'from it' (i.e., from God's face).
segments.paradiso-29-g73: 'da cui nulla si nasconde' = 'from whom nothing is hidden.' 'da cui' introduces the relative ('from whom/which'), 'nulla' = 'nothing,' and 'si nasconde' is reflexive/passive: 'is hidden.'
segments.paradiso-29-g74: 'però non hanno vedere interciso da novo obietto' = 'therefore they do not have their sight interrupted/cut off by any new object.' 'però' = 'therefore,' 'non hanno' = 'they do not have,' 'vedere interciso' = 'sight cut off/interrupted' (interciso = interrupted), 'da novo obietto' = 'by a new object' (i.e., no subsequent object interrupts their vision).
segments.paradiso-29-g75: 'e però non bisogna rememorar per concetto diviso' = 'and therefore it is not necessary to recall by a divided concept.' 'bisogna' is impersonal 'it is necessary,' 'rememorar' = 'to recall/remember,' and 'per concetto diviso' means 'by a broken/divided concept' — they do not need piecemeal concepts to recall what they already see.
segments.paradiso-29-g76: 'sì che là giù' = 'so that down there' (introducing the consequence for those below on earth). 'sì che' = 'so that/therefore,' and 'là giù' = 'down there/below.'
segments.paradiso-29-g77: 'non dormendo' is a gerund phrase 'not sleeping.' It describes the state 'down there' (people below are not literally sleeping but are not seeing clearly).
segments.paradiso-29-g78: 'si sogna' is an impersonal/personal construction 'one dreams' or 'people dream.' The reflexive/impersonal 'si' indicates an indefinite subject: 'it is dreamed' / 'people dream.'
segments.paradiso-29-g79: 'credendo e non credendo dicer vero' strings together gerunds: 'believing and not believing to say (it) true.' The sense: when people down there dream (without the direct vision), they alternately believe or disbelieve and yet claim to speak truth. 'dicer' = infinitive 'to say' and 'vero' = 'true.'
segments.paradiso-29-g80: 'ma ne l’uno è più colpa e più vergogna' = 'but in neither is there more guilt nor more shame.' 'ne l’uno' = 'in the one [case]' idiomatically meaning 'in either case'; 'è più colpa e più vergogna' denies that either belief or disbelief is more blameworthy — a balanced moral judgment. Note the negative contrast introduced by 'ma.'

sentences.paradiso-29-s5: Form and matter, joined and made pure, came forth to be things that had no flaw, like three arrows from a three‑stringed bow.
sentences.paradiso-29-s6: And as in glass, in amber or in crystal a ray shines so that from its coming to its being there is no interval at all, thus the three‑fold effect of its Lord in its being shone all at once, without distinction, in beginning to appear.
sentences.paradiso-29-s7: Order and structure were made concrete for the substances; and those were the summit in the world in which pure act was produced; pure potency held the lowest part; in the middle power and act bound themselves together with such bonds that they are never divided.
sentences.paradiso-29-s8: Jerome wrote there at length about the ages in which the angels were created before the other world was made; but this truth is written in many places by the writers of the Holy Spirit, and you will perceive it if you carefully observe; and reason also sees it to some extent, for it would not concede that the movers could be so without their own perfection.
sentences.paradiso-29-s17: You do not go down by a path by philosophizing: so much does the love of appearance and its thought carry you away!
sentences.paradiso-29-s18: And yet this here above behaves itself with less disdain than when the Divine Scripture is put aside or when it is perverted.
sentences.paradiso-29-s19: People do not think there how much blood it costs to sow it in the world, nor how much it pleases him who humbly approaches it.
sentences.paradiso-29-s20: In order to appear, each one devises and makes his own inventions; and those are passed over by the preachers, and the Gospel falls silent.
sentences.paradiso-29-s9: Now you know where and when these loves were created and how; so that three ardors in your desire are already extinguished.
sentences.paradiso-29-s10: Nor would you, by counting, reach twenty so quickly as the departure of part of the angels disturbed the source of your sustenance.
sentences.paradiso-29-s11: The other remained, and began this art that you discern, with such delight that it never parts from encircling.
sentences.paradiso-29-s12: The beginning of the fall was the accursed pride of him whom you saw constrained by all the weights of the world.
sentences.paradiso-29-s1: When both the children of Latona, covered by the Ram and by Libra, together make a band of the horizon — as far as the point where the zenith poises itself, until one and the other are parted from that girdle, changing hemisphere — so, with a face painted with a smile, Bëatrice fell silent, gazing fixedly at the point that had overcome me.
sentences.paradiso-29-s2: Then she began: "I speak, and I do not ask — what you wish to hear — because I have seen it there where every where and every when converge."
sentences.paradiso-29-s3: Not to gain any good for herself — she cannot be that — but so that her splendour might, by shining, say 'I subsist'; and in her eternity, outside of time, to comprehend beyond every other thing; as it pleased Him, the eternal Love opened itself into new loves.
sentences.paradiso-29-s4: Nor did she lie there almost torpid before; for neither before nor afterward did the discourse about God proceed over these waters.
sentences.paradiso-29-s29: The primal light, which radiates everywhere, is received in it in as many modes as there are splendors for whoever it appears to.
sentences.paradiso-29-s30: Therefore, since at the moment when it conceives the affection follows, the sweetness of loving seethes and warms differently in it.
sentences.paradiso-29-s31: Behold now the exalted thing and the breadth of eternal power; for, after so many mirrors have been made in which it is broken, each remains within itself as before.
sentences.paradiso-29-s25: But such a bird nests in the little beak, and if the common people saw it, they would see the indulgence on which it relies: because of which such foolishness grew on earth, that, without the proof of any testimony, people would rush to every promise.
sentences.paradiso-29-s26: From this the pig of Saint Anthony fattens, and many others who are even more piggish, paying money without minting.
sentences.paradiso-29-s27: But since we have digressed so much, let us now turn our eyes toward the straight road, so that the way may be shortened with time.
sentences.paradiso-29-s28: This nature so far extends itself in number that no mortal speech nor mortal thought has ever gone so far; and if you look at what is revealed by Daniel, you will see that in his thousands a determined number is hidden.
sentences.paradiso-29-s21: Someone says that the moon turned away during Christ’s Passion and interposed itself, so that the sun’s light did not reach down; but he lies, for the light hid itself from itself: therefore it made to the Spaniards and to the Indians the same kind of eclipse that it made to the Jews.
sentences.paradiso-29-s22: Florence does not have as many Lapi and Bindi as the number of such fabricated tales that each year are proclaimed on parchment here and there: so that the little sheep, who do not know, return from the pasture fed on wind, and their not seeing does not excuse them the harm.
sentences.paradiso-29-s23: Christ did not say to his first assembly, 'Go, and preach nonsense to the world'; but he gave them a true foundation; and that rang so loudly in his cheeks that, to fight in order to kindle the faith of the Gospel, they became shield and lance.
sentences.paradiso-29-s24: Now people go to preach armed with witticisms and with theatrical exits; provided they get a good laugh, they puff out the hood and ask for nothing more.
sentences.paradiso-29-s13: Those whom you see here were modest in acknowledging themselves to the goodness that had made them so quick to understand; therefore their sights were exalted by illuminating grace and by their merit, so that they have a firm and full will; and I do not wish there to be doubts, but be certain that receiving grace is meritorious according to how open the affection is.
sentences.paradiso-29-s14: By now, around this consistory you can contemplate much, if my words are gathered, without any other help.
sentences.paradiso-29-s15: But because on earth, through your schools, it is read that angelic nature is such that it understands and remembers and wills, I will still say, so that you may see clearly the truth that down there is confused, equivocating in such a reading.
sentences.paradiso-29-s16: These substances, since they were made joyful by the face of God, did not turn their gaze away from it, from whom nothing is hidden; therefore they do not have their sight cut off by any new object, and therefore it is not necessary to recall by a divided concept; so that down there, not sleeping, one dreams, believing and not believing to speak true; but in neither is there more guilt nor more shame.
